perturbation.original_text: 1. The undersigned will hold Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not, nor will it permit any agent, servant or employee to, copy, reproduce, sell, assign, license, market, transfer or otherwise dispose of, give or disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation including any partner or employee of Goldman Sachs who does not have a need to know the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The undersigned will hold Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not, nor will it permit any agent, servant or employee to, copy, reproduce, sell, assign, license, market, transfer or otherwise dispose of, give or disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation, including any partner or employee of Goldman Sachs, unless required by law.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception allowing disclosure of Confidential Information when 'required by law.' This creates an in-text contradiction because Section I states that the undersigned 'will not... disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation.' The addition of 'unless required by law' now introduces ambiguity and potential conflict about when disclosure is permissible and undermines the original strict confidentiality intended. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Non-Disclosure In connection with services now or in the future performed by the undersigned for Goldman, Sachs & Co. or for any subsidiary or affiliate of Goldman, Sachs & Co. (collectively called "Goldman Sachs"), the undersigned may have access to non-public information or materials describing or relating to Goldman Sachs or its clients, or third parties to whom Goldman Sachs has a duty of confidentiality, including materials describing or relating to the business affairs, policies or procedures of Goldman Sachs or its clients or such third parties; formulas; strategies; methods; processes; computer materials including source or object codes, data files, computer listings, computer programs, and other computer materials (regardless of the medium in which they are stored); or other information ("Confidential Information"). With respect to such Confidential Information, the undersigned acknowledges and agrees as follows: 1. The undersigned will hold Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not, nor will it permit any agent, servant or employee to, copy, reproduce, sell, assign, license, market, transfer or otherwise dispose of, give or disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation, including any partner or employee of Goldman Sachs, unless required by law. 2. Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned shall return to Goldman Sachs all copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, together, if requested by Goldman Sachs, with a certificate signed by the undersigned, in form and substance satisfactory to Goldman Sachs, to the effect that all such Confidential Information has been returned.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section I strictly prohibits any disclosure of Confidential Information to any person or entity. The changed text introduces an exception ('unless required by law'), which directly contradicts the original strict prohibition. The contradicted text is the same section where the change was made. This exception creates ambiguity and a potential conflict regarding when disclosure is permissible, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned shall return to Goldman Sachs all copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, together, if requested by Goldman Sachs, with a certificate signed by the undersigned, in form and substance satisfactory to Goldman Sachs, to the effect that all such Confidential Information has been returned.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned may retain copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, if such retention is deemed necessary by the undersigned.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the undersigned to retain copies of confidential information if 'deemed necessary.' This directly contradicts the original clause which mandates the return of ALL copies upon termination of services. This creates an in-text contradiction within section I, as the amended text now permits something explicitly forbidden in the earlier part of the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Non-Disclosure In connection with services now or in the future performed by the undersigned for Goldman, Sachs & Co. or for any subsidiary or affiliate of Goldman, Sachs & Co. (collectively called "Goldman Sachs"), the undersigned may have access to non-public information or materials describing or relating to Goldman Sachs or its clients, or third parties to whom Goldman Sachs has a duty of confidentiality, including materials describing or relating to the business affairs, policies or procedures of Goldman Sachs or its clients or such third parties; formulas; strategies; methods; processes; computer materials including source or object codes, data files, computer listings, computer programs, and other computer materials (regardless of the medium in which they are stored); or other information ("Confidential Information"). With respect to such Confidential Information, the undersigned acknowledges and agrees as follows: 1. The undersigned will hold Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not, nor will it permit any agent, servant or employee to, copy, reproduce, sell, assign, license, market, transfer or otherwise dispose of, give or disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation including any partner or employee of Goldman Sachs who does not have a need to know the Confidential Information. 2. Upon the termination of the services to be performed by the undersigned (or earlier if requested by Goldman Sachs), the undersigned may retain copies of documents, papers or other material relating to Goldman Sachs or obtained or developed in the course of performing services for Goldman Sachs, or containing or derived from Confidential Information which are in the undersigned's possession, if such retention is deemed necessary by the undersigned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 2, allowing the undersigned to retain confidential information 'if deemed necessary'. This directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text, which implicitly requires all confidential information to be returned upon termination of services, as stated in the original clause in Section I. The contradicted text explicitly states that the undersigned 'may retain copies', which is the direct opposite of returning ALL copies. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned agrees to provide any assistance necessary to Goldman Sachs in investigating any illegal or fraudulent activities, security breaches or similar situations.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned agrees to provide any assistance necessary to Goldman Sachs in investigating any illegal or fraudulent activities, security breaches or similar situations, provided that such assistance does not conflict with their other professional obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'provided that such assistance does not conflict with their other professional obligations,' the change introduces a qualifier that weakens the original obligation. This creates an in-text contradiction because it implies the undersigned *may not* provide assistance if it conflicts with other obligations, directly contradicting the original statement where the undersigned agrees to provide *any* assistance. It creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. Non-Employment The undersigned affirms that the undersigned is not an employee of Goldman Sachs for any purpose and that the undersigned is not entitled to exercise any rights, or seek any benefit, accruing to the regular employees of Goldman Sachs by virtue of the services rendered by the undersigned to Goldman Sachs or otherwise. The undersigned agrees to provide any assistance necessary to Goldman Sachs in investigating any illegal or fraudulent activities, security breaches or similar situations, provided that such assistance does not conflict with their other professional obligations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the undersigned agrees to provide 'any assistance necessary.' The changed text adds 'provided that such assistance does not conflict with their other professional obligations.' This introduces a qualifier that weakens the original obligation, creating a contradiction. The contradiction occurs because the original implies *unconditional* assistance, while the changed text allows the undersigned to *potentially refuse* assistance, which creates a contradiction in the text. The contradicted text is the same text as the changed text, so this is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations created by this Agreement shall survive the termination of the services of the undersigned.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations created by this Agreement shall survive the termination of the services of the undersigned, except for the obligation of non-disclosure, which shall terminate immediately.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for the obligation of non-disclosure, which shall terminate immediately', there is a contradiction where the obligations are meant to survive the termination of the services but then the clause for non-disclosure is terminating right away. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the first sentence says *all* obligations survive, but the added clause explicitly terminates the non-disclosure obligation. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned agrees that in connection with performing the services contemplated by this Agreement, he or she may be subject to a background check, including employers, education, credit, criminal public record, drug screen or other checking or testing, and the undersigned consents to the foregoing. The undersigned hereby releases Goldman Sachs, its employees and agents from any and all liability or claims arising from such checking and testing and the use and reporting of the results thereof except for those arising due to the negligence of Goldman Sachs. The obligations created by this Agreement shall survive the termination of the services of the undersigned, except for the obligation of non-disclosure, which shall terminate immediately. The undersigned acknowledges that any violation, breach or other failure on the undersigned's part to strictly comply with this Agreement could materially adversely affect Goldman Sachs and its business, thus giving rise to suit for monetary damages and/or injunctive relief for such violation, breach or other failure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all obligations created by the Agreement survive termination. The changed text introduces an exception to this rule, stating that the obligation of non-disclosure terminates immediately. This directly contradicts the initial statement that *all* obligations survive, thus creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction as it relates to the contradictory text, which contains both the original and changed text.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledges that any violation, breach or other failure on the undersigned's part to strictly comply with this Agreement could materially adversely affect Goldman Sachs and its business, thus giving rise to suit for monetary damages and/or injunctive relief for such violation, breach or other failure.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledges that any violation, breach or other failure on the undersigned's part to strictly comply with this Agreement could materially adversely affect Goldman Sachs and its business; however, Goldman Sachs agrees to first attempt mediation before pursuing legal action for any alleged violation.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'however, Goldman Sachs agrees to first attempt mediation before pursuing legal action for any alleged violation', the action to seek monetary damages and/or injunctive relief for such violation is now contradicted by the requirement to attempt mediation before legal action. The contradiction lies in the conditional legal action, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned agrees that in connection with performing the services contemplated by this Agreement, he or she may be subject to a background check, including employers, education, credit, criminal public record, drug screen or other checking or testing, and the undersigned consents to the foregoing. The undersigned hereby releases Goldman Sachs, its employees and agents from any and all liability or claims arising from such checking and testing and the use and reporting of the results thereof except for those arising due to the negligence of Goldman Sachs. The obligations created by this Agreement shall survive the termination of the services of the undersigned. The undersigned acknowledges that any violation, breach or other failure on the undersigned's part to strictly comply with this Agreement could materially adversely affect Goldman Sachs and its business; however, Goldman Sachs agrees to first attempt mediation before pursuing legal action for any alleged violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any violation allows Goldman Sachs to pursue monetary damages and/or injunctive relief. The changed text adds a clause requiring Goldman Sachs to attempt mediation before pursuing legal action for any alleged violation. This contradicts the original statement by imposing a condition (mediation) before legal action, thus restricting the immediate right to seek damages or injunctive relief. The contradicted text contains the changed text within it, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Background Check and Testing The undersigned agrees that in connection with performing the services contemplated by this Agreement, he or she may be subject to a background check, including employers, education, credit, criminal public record, drug screen or other checking or testing, and the undersigned consents to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Background Check and Testing The undersigned agrees that in connection with performing the services contemplated by this Agreement, he or she may be subject to a background check, including employers, education, credit, criminal public record, drug screen or other checking or testing; however, the undersigned reserves the right to refuse any specific test if it violates their personal beliefs.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause, 'however, the undersigned reserves the right to refuse any specific test if it violates their personal beliefs', introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause states the undersigned *consents* to the background check and testing. The new clause allows them to *refuse* tests based on personal beliefs. This creates an in-text contradiction within section IV and introduces ambiguity about the enforceability of the background check requirement and undermines its original intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned agrees that in connection with performing the services contemplated by this Agreement, he or she may be subject to a background check, including employers, education, credit, criminal public record, drug screen or other checking or testing; however, the undersigned reserves the right to refuse any specific test if it violates their personal beliefs. The undersigned hereby releases Goldman Sachs, its employees and agents from any and all liability or claims arising from such checking and testing and the use and reporting of the results thereof except for those arising due to the negligence of Goldman Sachs. The obligations created by this Agreement shall survive the termination of the services of the undersigned. The undersigned acknowledges that any violation, breach or other failure on the undersigned's part to strictly comply with this Agreement could materially adversely affect Goldman Sachs and its business, thus giving rise to suit for monetary damages and/or injunctive relief for such violation, breach or other failure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within the agreement. The original text implies consent to background checks, while the changed text allows the undersigned to refuse specific tests based on personal beliefs. This directly conflicts with the prior implied consent, creating an inconsistency and potential unenforceability of the background check requirement. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition, making the obligations unclear.
